body:
## Context
Greenfield note-taking app. Users will edit notes across multiple devices; the sync service keeps a user's notes consistent across those devices and handles offline edits.

## Goal
A service that lets clients push local note changes and pull remote changes since a known point, reconciling concurrent edits.

## Scope
- A sync endpoint that accepts a batch of client-side note changes (creates/updates/deletes) with timestamps/versions.
- Return the set of server-side changes the client hasn't seen (delta sync via a cursor/updated-since marker).
- A conflict-resolution policy (start simple, e.g. last-write-wins by version, and document it).
- Per-note version or updated-at tracking to support deltas (coordinate with #2's data model).
- Operates only on the authenticated user's notes (depends on #1).

## Out of scope (for now)
- Real-time push (websockets), CRDT-based merge, multi-user shared notes. File follow-ups if needed.

## Acceptance criteria
- A client can push local changes and have them persisted.
- A client can pull all changes since a given cursor and receive only newer changes.
- Concurrent edits resolve per the documented policy without data loss of the winning version.
- Sync is scoped to the authenticated user.
- Tests cover push, pull-with-cursor, and a conflict scenario.

## Dependencies
Depends on auth (#1) and the notes data model (#2).
title: Notes sync service

body:
## Context
Greenfield note-taking app. Once users can authenticate (#1), they need to manage notes. This service is the core domain of the app.

## Goal
A RESTful CRUD API for notes, scoped to the authenticated user.

## Scope
- Create a note (title + body, timestamps).
- List the current user's notes; get a single note by id.
- Update a note; delete a note.
- All endpoints require a valid auth token (depends on #1) and only operate on notes owned by the caller.
- Persistence for notes.

## Out of scope (for now)
- Sharing/collaboration, tags, search, attachments. File follow-ups if needed.

## Acceptance criteria
- Authenticated user can create, read (list + single), update, and delete their own notes.
- A user cannot read or modify another user's notes (authorization enforced).
- Unauthenticated requests are rejected.
- Validation errors (e.g. missing title) return clear 4xx responses.
- Unit/integration tests cover each operation plus the ownership/authorization boundary.

## Dependencies
Depends on the auth service (#1) for identifying the current user.
title: Notes CRUD API

body:
## Context
Greenfield note-taking app. The first foundational capability is user authentication: notes and sync are per-user, so every other service depends on a user identity and a way to authenticate requests.

## Goal
Provide a self-contained authentication service that lets a user sign up, log in, and make authenticated requests.

## Scope
- User registration (email + password), with passwords stored hashed (never plaintext).
- Login that issues a session token (JWT or equivalent).
- Middleware/util to validate a token and resolve the current user.
- Input validation and sensible error responses.
- Persistence for user records (start with a simple store; can evolve).

## Out of scope (for now)
- OAuth/social login, password reset emails, MFA. File follow-ups if needed.

## Acceptance criteria
- A new user can register and receive a success response; duplicate email is rejected.
- A registered user can log in with correct credentials and receives a token; wrong credentials are rejected.
- A protected endpoint rejects requests without a valid token and accepts them with one.
- Passwords are stored hashed; tokens are signed.
- Unit tests cover register, login (success + failure), and token validation.

## Notes
This is the foundation other services (notes CRUD, sync) build on. Keep the public interface (how other services verify a user) clean and documented.
title: User authentication service
